items.title: Wall Street Brunch: Big Banks Kick Off Earnings Season
items.description: Big banks and Netflix to report as Q2 reporting heats up. June CPI seen rising. Amazon sees biggest Prime Day

items.title: Big Banks, Netflix, Pepsico, GE Aerospace: Earnings this week
items.description: A fresh earnings season is kicking off this coming week, with Big Banks leading the way on Tuesday and Wednesday. Asking for a Trend host Josh Lipton goes over the top earnings reports to look for, starting Tuesday, July 15. On Tuesday, JPMorgan (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), and Citigroup (C) are reporting earnings. On Wednesday, Bank of America (BAC), Morgan Stanley (MS), and Goldman Sachs (GS) will release their reports. Banks are expected to overall report trading revenue gains. On Thursday, Netflix (NFLX), GE Aerospace (GE), and Pepsico (PEP) will be publishing their results. Netflix's second quarter earnings will be out after the markets close, following overall outperformance this year despite some slowing momentum. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Asking for a Trend here.

items.title: Xi’s Price-War Campaign Creates a Buzz in China’s Stock Market

items.title: Worst Spate of Downgrades Since 2021 Signals Pain
items.description: (Bloomberg) -- Credit rating downgrades are becoming more frequent, the latest sign that companies are starting to perform worse and raising fresh questions about whether corporate debt valuations should be as high as they are.
